title: New Approach to Detect and Isolate Rhamnogalacturonan-II in Arabidopsis thaliana Seed Mucilage
author: Dayan Sanhueza

steps.title: Pyridine:acetic acid:water (PyAW, 1:10:200 v/v/v), 0
steps.instruction: Pyridine:acetic acid:water (PyAW, 1:10:200 v/v/v), 0.25% chlorobutanol Example for 100 mL: Reagent Quantity/Volume Pyridine 47 μL Acetic acid 4.74 mL Water 9.47 mL Chlorobutanol 0.25 g

steps.title: Pyridine:acetic acid:water (PyAW, 1:1:98 v/v/v), 0
steps.instruction: Pyridine:acetic acid:water (PyAW, 1:1:98 v/v/v), 0.25% chlorobutanol Example for 1 L: Reagent Quantity/Volume Pyridine 10 mL Acetic acid 10 mL Water 980 mL Chlorobutanol 2.5 g

steps.title: Solution A Add 0
steps.instruction: Solution A Add 0.5 g of Borax decahydrate to 100 mL of sulfuric acid.

steps.title: Solution B Add 0
steps.instruction: Solution B Add 0.4 g of NaOH to 10 mL of water; then, add 0.015 g of 3-hydroxybiphenyl.

steps.title: Galacturonic acid stock solution Prepare a 0
steps.instruction: Galacturonic acid stock solution Prepare a 0.1 mg/mL stock solution of galacturonic acid (GalA) by dissolving 1 mg of GalA in 10 mL of MilliQ water.

steps.title: Tris-HCl 1
steps.instruction: Tris-HCl 1.5 M, pH 8.8 To prepare 1 L, weigh 181.65 g of Tris base and dissolve it in 700 mL of MilliQ water. Add 31 mL of hydrochloric acid, and then adjust the pH to the desired value using 1 M HCl. Finally, bring the volume up to 1 L with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Ammonium persulfate 10% (APS) To prepare...
steps.instruction: Ammonium persulfate 10% (APS) To prepare 10 mL, add 1 g of ammonium persulfate to 10 mL of MilliQ water.

steps.title: Polyacrylamide gel 26
steps.instruction: Polyacrylamide gel 26.4% Example for 5 mL (one mini gel, using 0.75 mm spacers): Reagent Quantity Tris base 1.5 M pH 8.8 834 μL Acrylamide/Bis acrylamide (29:
40% 3.33 mL APS 46.7 μL TEMED 3.9 μL Water 834 μL

steps.title: Running buffer for PAGE Example for 1 L (10×)
steps.instruction: Running buffer for PAGE Example for 1 L (10×): Reagent Quantity Tris base 60.57 g Glycine 28.53 g

steps.title: Loading buffer To prepare 20 mL of buffer, dissolve 1
steps.instruction: Loading buffer To prepare 20 mL of buffer, dissolve 1.53 g of Tris in 7 mL of water, adjust the pH to 6.8 with 1 M HCl, and make up to 10 mL with water. Then, add 0.05 g of bromophenol blue and 10 mL of glycerol.

steps.title: Fixing solution Example for 1 L
steps.instruction: Fixing solution Example for 1 L: Reagent Quantity/Volume Ethanol 400 mL Acetic acid 100 mL Water 500 mL

steps.title: 400 µM solution of sodium thiosulfate Add 63
steps.instruction: 400 µM solution of sodium thiosulfate Add 63.2 mg of sodium thiosulfate to 1 L water.

steps.title: 6 mM silver nitrate and 10...
steps.instruction: 6 mM silver nitrate and 10 mM formaldehyde solution Add 0.102 g of silver nitrate and 75.2 μL of 37% formaldehyde to 100 mL of water. Note: This solution must be freshly prepared.

steps.title: Developing solution Add 29
steps.instruction: Developing solution Add 29.68 g of sodium carbonate, 20 mL of 400 µM sodium thiosulfate solution (Recipe 13), and 4.8 mL of 37% formaldehyde to 975.2 mL of water.

steps.title: Stopping solution Add 40 g of...
steps.instruction: Stopping solution Add 40 g of Tris to 980 mL of water, then complete to 1 L with 20 mL of glacial acetic acid. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Bio-Gel P-30 (Bio-Rad, catalog number:
steps.instruction: Bio-Gel P-30 (Bio-Rad, catalog number:

steps.title: 96-well plate (Nest, catalog number:
steps.instruction: 96-well plate (Nest, catalog number:

steps.title: Epoch 96-well plate absorbance reader (Agilent...
steps.instruction: Epoch 96-well plate absorbance reader (Agilent BioTek Epoch Microplate Spectrophotometer, catalog number: 11-120-572)

steps.title: Weigh approximately 50 mg of dry seeds
steps.instruction: Weigh approximately 50 mg of dry seeds.

steps.title: Isolate soluble mucilage by incubating the...
steps.instruction: Isolate soluble mucilage by incubating the seeds in 4 mL of MilliQ water for 2 h with agitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 3,500× g for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 3,500× g for 5 min.

steps.title: Recover the supernatant, immediately boil it...
steps.instruction: Recover the supernatant, immediately boil it at 100 °C for 5 min, and immediately store it at -20 °C.

steps.title: Freeze-dry the sample
steps.instruction: Freeze-dry the sample.

steps.title: Resuspend the dried mucilage in 500 μL of 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend the dried mucilage in 500 μL of 0.5 M Na 2 CO 3 and incubate overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Neutralise the Na 2 CO 3...
steps.instruction: Neutralise the Na 2 CO 3 with approximately 50 μL of glacial acetic acid, monitoring the reaction by observing the cessation of fizzing. Note: The reaction between Na 2 CO 3 and acetic acid (CH 3 COOH) is an acid–base reaction that produces sodium acetate, water, and carbon dioxide, which is released as a gas, visible as bubbling or fizzing.

steps.title: Precipitate the mucilage by adding two volumes (1
steps.instruction: Precipitate the mucilage by adding two volumes (1.1 mL) of absolute ethanol.

steps.title: Pellet the mucilage by centrifuging at...
steps.instruction: Pellet the mucilage by centrifuging at 3,500× g for 5 min. Note: Steps 4 to 9 can be visualised in the Video 1 .

steps.title: Rinse the pellet twice with 80%...
steps.instruction: Rinse the pellet twice with 80% ethanol, followed by a final rinse with absolute ethanol. Note: Rinsing is performed by vortexing the sample.

steps.title: Resuspend the mucilage in 2 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the mucilage in 2 mL of PyAW, 1:10:200 v/v/v containing 0.25% chlorobutanol, and digest overnight at 25 °C with agitation using 1.1 U endo-polygalacturonase.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 3,500× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 3,500× g for 5 min to pellet any undigested material.

steps.title: Load the entire supernatant (2 mL)...
steps.instruction: Load the entire supernatant (2 mL) remaining after centrifugation onto a Bio-Gel P-30 column (2.5 × 57 cm). Set the flow rate of the column to 1 mL per min.

steps.title: Use PyAW, 1:1:98 v/v/v containing 0
steps.instruction: Use PyAW, 1:1:98 v/v/v containing 0.25% chlorobutanol as the eluent.

steps.title: Discard the first 10 mL of...
steps.instruction: Discard the first 10 mL of eluent, then collect 60 fractions of 5 mL each. Video

steps.title: Key steps in mucilage processing B
steps.instruction: Key steps in mucilage processing B. Uronic acid quantification

steps.title: Add 20 μL of the sample...
steps.instruction: Add 20 μL of the sample fraction to 100 μL of solution A.

steps.title: Mix the solution by vortexing, then...
steps.instruction: Mix the solution by vortexing, then incubate at 100 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: Transfer the entire volume (120 μL) to a 96-well plate
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire volume (120 μL) to a 96-well plate.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance at 520 nm
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance at 520 nm.

steps.title: Add 2 μL of solution B...
steps.instruction: Add 2 μL of solution B and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance again at 520 nm
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance again at 520 nm.

steps.title: Plot a graph of the amount...
steps.instruction: Plot a graph of the amount of GalA (µg) against the fraction number.

steps.title: The concentration is determined using the...
steps.instruction: The concentration is determined using the calibration curve described in Table 1 . Note: This enables identification of the peak corresponding to the elution zone of RG-II ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Uronic acid quantification
steps.instruction: Uronic acid quantification. As expected for a mucilage sample, the majority of the material corresponds to rhamnogalacturonan-I (RG-I) (between fractions 11 and 19). Based on our experience with other types of samples, rhamnogalacturonan-II (RG-II) elutes between fractions 21 and

steps.title: Therefore, we proceed to concentrate these...
steps.instruction: Therefore, we proceed to concentrate these fractions using a speed vacuum. C. Galacturonic acid standard curve

steps.title: Prepare 80 μL of each GalA...
steps.instruction: Prepare 80 μL of each GalA concentration in the calibration series. Table

steps.title: Calibration series of GalA concentration GalA...
steps.instruction: Calibration series of GalA concentration GalA amount (µg) GalA stock solution (µL) MilliQ water (µL) 0 0 20 0.5 5 15 1 10 10 1.5 15 5 2 20 0

steps.title: Repeat steps B1–7, but replace samples for each GalA concentration
steps.instruction: Repeat steps B1–7, but replace samples for each GalA concentration. Note: We recommend using three replicates. D. Sample concentration

steps.title: Pool the fractions corresponding to tubes...
steps.instruction: Pool the fractions corresponding to tubes 21–29 and dry them using a speed vac at room temperature.

steps.title: Once dry, resuspend the sample in...
steps.instruction: Once dry, resuspend the sample in 200 μL of MilliQ water.

steps.title: Quantify the GalA concentration by repeating steps B1–
steps.instruction: Quantify the GalA concentration by repeating steps B1–

steps.title: Prepare a 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a 0.1 µg/μL solution. E. Polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (PAGE)

steps.title: Cast the PAGE gel following the instructions provided
steps.instruction: Cast the PAGE gel following the instructions provided.

steps.title: Mix 8 µL of the 0
steps.instruction: Mix 8 µL of the 0.1 μg/μL RG-II solution with 2 μL of loading buffer and load the sample into the gel.

steps.title: Run the gel at 200 V for 75 min
steps.instruction: Run the gel at 200 V for 75 min.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min in the fixing solution
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min in the fixing solution.

steps.title: Wash three times, 1 min each, with MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Wash three times, 1 min each, with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Incubate for 1 min in the...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1 min in the 400 μM sodium thiosulfate solution.

steps.title: Wash three times, 1 min each, with MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Wash three times, 1 min each, with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Incubate for 20 min in the...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 20 min in the solution containing 6 mM silver nitrate and 10 mM formaldehyde.

steps.title: Wash twice with MilliQ water, 20 s each
steps.instruction: Wash twice with MilliQ water, 20 s each.

steps.title: Incubate with the developing solution for...
steps.instruction: Incubate with the developing solution for 2–10 min or until bands become visible.

steps.title: Stop the reaction by adding the stopping solution
steps.instruction: Stop the reaction by adding the stopping solution.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol was used for the detection and isolation of RG-II from Vasconcellea pubescens seed mucilage (Sanhueza et al. [8] https://doi.org/10.3389/fpls.2024.1380533 ). It was also applied for the isolation of RG-I from Arabidopsis seed mucilage (Saez-Aguayo et al. [9] https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tcsw.2024.100134 ).
